original_abstract: This article describes Yudian Wahyudi's thoughts regarding Maqasid al-Syari’ah as a method of solving contemporary problems in Indonesia. The primary data of this research was obtained from Yudian's writings about Maqasid al-Syari’ah. The secondary data were obtained from authoritative, up-to-date, and relevant literature sources. This study finds an Indonesian Maqasid al-Syari’ah theory, which is different from the Classical and Contemporary Maqasid al-Syari’ah. Indonesian Maqasid al-Syari’ah wants to harmonize between Maqasid al-Syari’ah (five primary needs or zharuriyyat al-khams) and IndonesianMaqasid (based on Pancasila). Maqasid al-Syari’ah Yudian's idea rests on four pillars, namely: tauhidul 'ulum (unity of knowledge) between Ayat Qur’aniyah, Kauniyah, and Insaniyah (zharuriyat ats-tsalasah) as the embodiment of the slogan al-Ruju' ila al-Qur'an wa al-Sunnah (Return to the Qur'an and Sunnah), the theory of pairing in Islamic law (ilahi-wad'i, absolute-relative, universal-local, eternal-temporary, and literal-meaning), method (Maqasid al -Syari'ah as an analytical knife or point of view to read the reality that is around us), and matters related to Indonesia. Meanwhile, Maqasid Indonesia is based on the five basic values of the goals of the Indonesian nation as embodied in Pancasila (Salam Pancasila): divinity (hifdz al-din), humanity (hifdz al-nafs), unity (hifdz al-nasl), deliberation and wisdom (hifdz al-’aql), and social justice (hifdz al-mal). The results of this study can have implications for the development and alignment factors of the Maqasid al-Syari’ah theory, which is unique to Indonesia. Indonesian Maqasid al-Syari’ah is a strengthening of the foundation of Usul al-Fiqh and Indonesian Fiqh.
humanized_abstract: Yudian Wahyudi as one of Sharia Studies lecturers who teaches Maqasid al-Syari’ah at Sunan Kalamar Islamic University of Bondowoso (Unwesbo) explained his way of thought regarding Maqasid al-Syari’ah that can be used as a manner and method in solving current Indonesian problems. In this research, the main data is derived from the work of Yudian and arranged systematically. Supporting data is also derived from some relevant, modern and complete literature sources. The finding of this research explains that there is an Indonesian Maqasid al-Syari’ah which does not coincide with the Classical Maqasid al-Syari’ah and the Contemporary Maqasid al-Syari’ah. The Indonesian Maqasid al-Syari’ah is formed by combining the Maqasid al-Syari’ah (five necessarys or zharuriyyat al-khams) with the Maqasid Indonesia derived from Pancasila. The ideas of Yudian regarding Maqasid al-Syari’ah are developed based on four principles: first, tauhidul ‘ulum between Ayat Qur’aniyah, Kauniyah, and Insaniyah (zharuriyat ats-tsalasah) as the realization of al-Ruju' ila al-Qur'an wa al-Sunnah and the theory of pairing in Islamic law (ilahi-wad'i, absolute-relative, universal-local, eternal-temporary, and literal-meaning); second, method (Maqasid al-Syari'ah as an analytical knife or as a viewpoint to see the reality of life around us) and third, matters related to Indonesia. Maqasid Indonesia is derived from five basic values which are the aims of the Indonesian nation as enshrined in Pancasila known as Salam Pancasila, namely: divinity (hifdz al-din), humanity (hifdz al-nafs), unity (hifdz al-nasl), deliberation and wisdom (hifdz al-’aql), and social justice (hifdz al-mal). The conclusion of this research is that there is an Indonesian Maqasid al-Syari’ah concept which can be used as a reference in determining the strength and direction of the factor relating to Maqasid al-Syari’ah in Indonesia, so that it can enrich and strengthen the foundational theory of Usul al-Fiqh and Indonesian Fiqh.

The finding of this research explains that there is an Indonesian Maqasid al-Syari’ah which does not coincide with the Classical Maqasid al-Syari’ah and the Contemporary Maqasid al-Syari’ah. The Indonesian Maqasid al-Syari’ah is formed by combining the Maqasid al-Syari’ah (five necessarys or zharuriyyat al-khams) with the Maqasid Indonesia derived from Pancasila. The ideas of Yudian regarding Maqasid al-Syari’ah are developed based on four principles: first, tauhidul ‘ulum between Ayat Qur’aniyah, Kauniyah, and Insaniyah (zharuriyat ats-tsalasah) as the realization of al-Ruju' ila al-Qur'an wa al-Sunnah and the theory of pairing in Islamic law (ilahi-wad'i, absolute-relative, universal-local, eternal-temporary, and literal-meaning); second, method (Maqasid al-Syari'ah as an analytical knife or as a viewpoint to see the reality of life around us) and third, matters related to Indonesia. Maqasid Indonesia is derived from five basic values which are the aims of the Indonesian nation as enshrined in Pancasila known as Salam Pancasila, namely: divinity (hifdz al-din), humanity (hifdz al-nafs), unity (hifdz al-nasl), deliberation and wisdom (hifdz al-’aql), and social justice (hifdz al-mal).
